skill.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
skill.description: Coordinate multiple agents to investigate independent coding failures in parallel while keeping each agent scoped to its own files and tests.
skill.summary: Dispatch scoped agents for independent debugging work.
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external command patterns and one weak cryptography pattern, but those matches are false positives in Markdown examples and front matter text. The remaining risk is contextual: the skill permits Bash and delegated agents, so users need clear scoping before parallel work starts.

medium_findings.title: Broad Tool Permissions for Parallel Debugging
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash and Task in allowed tools and directs users to dispatch multiple agents. This can run shell commands and change files if users provide broad scopes, but the instructions constrain agents to assigned files and do not show malicious commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tools and parallel task template are explicit. I found no command payload, network endpoint, or secret access, so the risk is contextual.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Matches Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The fifteen static external command matches are inside fenced Markdown examples and task descriptions. They are not Ruby backtick execution and do not contain runnable shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched regions are fenced documentation blocks and tables. The file contains no Ruby code or shell backtick execution in those regions.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Match Is Front Matter Text
low_findings.description: The high static weak cryptography finding at line 3 is a false positive in the description text. No evidence found for cryptographic code, weak algorithms, key handling, or encryption behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain skill metadata describing independent failures and dependencies. It does not reference or implement any cryptographic algorithm.

content.value_statement: Independent failures can waste time when handled one after another. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code assign scoped agents to separate failures and merge the results.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when multiple failures are independent enough for parallel investigation.
Defines separate agent scopes by file, directory, test, or subsystem.
Provides a prompt structure for assigning goals and constraints to each agent.
Guides consolidation of root causes, edits, and test results after agent work.
Calls out common mistakes such as broad scope, missing context, and unclear outputs.
limitations: It is not suitable when failures share one root cause or require global system context.
It depends on the host assistant's Task, file, and shell tool permissions.
It does not guarantee conflict-free edits if users assign overlapping scopes.
It provides workflow guidance, not a standalone scheduler or test runner.

use_cases.title: Split Independent Test Failures
use_cases.description: Assign separate agents to unrelated failing test files, then combine root causes and test results.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Subsystem Debugging
use_cases.description: Investigate authentication, routing, and database issues in parallel when each subsystem has clear ownership.

use_cases.title: Speed Up Maintenance Tasks
use_cases.description: Use bounded agent prompts to handle several small defects without mixing unrelated code changes.

prompt_templates.title: Find Parallel Candidates
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner triage for a failing test run.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these failures and identify which ones are independent enough for parallel agents. Explain any dependencies before assigning work.

prompt_templates.title: Create Scoped Agent Tasks
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare clear assignments before dispatching agents.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create one agent task per independent failure. Include scope, goal, constraints, and expected output for each task.

prompt_templates.title: Dispatch and Merge Results
prompt_templates.scenario: Run multiple investigations and consolidate their findings.
prompt_templates.prompt: Dispatch agents for these independent scopes. After they finish, summarize root causes, file changes, tests run, and any conflicts.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Parallel Fix Quality
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review after several agents made changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the parallel agent results for overlapping edits, inconsistent assumptions, missing tests, and remaining risk. Recommend follow-up fixes.

output_examples.input: Three unrelated test files fail after a dependency update.
output: A failure matrix that marks each test file as independent or dependent.
Three scoped agent assignments with directories, goals, constraints, and expected results.
A final merge summary covering causes, fixes, tests, and conflicts.

output_examples.input: A lead wants to divide auth, API, and database failures across agents.
output: Separate work packages for each subsystem.
Clear limits that prevent agents from editing outside their assigned files.
A concise integration report after all agent investigations complete.

output_examples.input: Several maintenance defects appear small but may overlap.
output: A recommendation to keep overlapping defects in one agent when shared context is required.
A list of safe parallel tasks and tasks that should remain sequential.

best_practices: Dispatch agents only after confirming the failures do not share a likely root cause.
Give each agent explicit files, tests, constraints, and expected output.
Review all results together before accepting edits or declaring the issue fixed.
anti_patterns: Do not assign overlapping directories to separate agents without a merge plan.
Do not use parallel agents when one system-level change may fix every failure.
Do not omit test expectations or output requirements from agent prompts.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it when you have at least three independent failures that can be investigated without shared state.

faq.question: Can it fix code directly?
faq.answer: It provides the workflow and prompt structure. Actual edits depend on the assistant tools available in the environment.

faq.question: Does it require Claude Code?
faq.answer: No. It is written for Claude-style agent dispatch and is also useful with Codex workflows.

faq.question: What is the main safety concern?
faq.answer: Parallel agents can make conflicting edits if their scopes overlap or their constraints are unclear.

faq.question: Can I use it for one failing test?
faq.answer: No. A single failure is usually better handled by one focused investigation.

faq.question: How should I review the results?
faq.answer: Compare root causes, changed files, tests run, and unresolved risks before merging the work.
